title: Chat → MCP Tools → Manual Obs Popup → Report → PDF + Redis-Backed SSE Replay
description: Implements end-to-end chat-driven report generation with Redis Streams-backed SSE replay, MCP tool orchestration, pending manual-observation lifecycle, report listing/detail/versioning/regeneration, and a dedicated PDF worker that renders sanitized HTML to PDF and updates report readiness.

env_vars_required.description: PostgreSQL 16 primary DB connection used by Next.js app and PDF worker metadata updates.
Redis for BullMQ queues, Redis Streams replay buffer, and pub/sub channels pending-obs/report-pdf notifications.
Cloudflare R2 account for presigned URL generation and object operations.
R2 access key for HTML/PDF uploads and reads.
R2 secret key for object operations.
Bucket storing report HTML snapshots and generated PDFs.
Grok streaming generation and tool-driven response orchestration.
OpenAI text-embedding-3-small for search_evidence hybrid retrieval path reuse.
Reads/writes user/company/client scoped style preferences for generation/regeneration.
Auth.js origin for JWT session handling.
Optional direct health-check URL for PDF worker; queue communication still uses Redis.
Frontend base URL for internal links and UI navigation.
Chromium executable path in PDF worker container.
Prevents Puppeteer from downloading bundled Chromium in container builds.

files.purpose: Create and list user-scoped chat sessions.

sections.label: Handlers
sections.content: Implements POST /api/chat/sessions with empty body, creates chat_session row with title 'Nouvelle conversation', returns { session_id, created_at }. Implements GET /api/chat/sessions paginated listing sorted by updated_at DESC default limit 20 with message_count aggregation per session.

files.purpose: Delete a chat session and cascade messages.

sections.label: Delete
sections.content: Implements DELETE /api/chat/sessions/{id}; validates ownership (ChatSession.user_id = ctx.userId), relies on FK ON DELETE CASCADE for chat_message cleanup, returns 204 No Content.

files.purpose: Create a user message and dispatch asynchronous agent turn.

sections.label: Post Message
sections.content: Implements POST /api/chat/sessions/{id}/messages with body { text }. Validates 1..4000 chars and session ownership, inserts chat_message role=user type=user_text content={text}, updates chat_session.title on first message to 60-char truncation, updates updated_at, dispatches agent loop asynchronously without awaiting, returns 202 { message_id }.

files.purpose: Serve persistent text/event-stream with replay support.

sections.label: SSE
sections.content: Implements GET stream. Reads Last-Event-ID header, replays missed events from Redis Stream via XRANGE, subscribes to new events via XREAD BLOCK 0 COUNT 10, forwards SSE frames with stream ID as id, unsubscribes on disconnect, leaves stream key alive until TTL expiry after done.

files.purpose: Poll pending manual observation status.

sections.label: Pending State
sections.content: Implements GET returning { pending_id, status, observation_id, created_at, expires_at }. Returns 404 { code: 'pending_not_found' } for missing or expired records.

files.purpose: Confirm pending observation and create manual approved observation + attestation.

sections.label: Confirm
sections.content: Implements POST confirm. Validates terminal-state guard (must be pending), validates full popup schema (label, normalized_key, value, unit, data_type, time_behavior, periodic dates, optional category_id/source_reference/note), inserts attestation_record and manual observation status=approved, updates pending status=confirmed + observation_id, publishes pending-obs:{pending_id} message with { status:'confirmed', observation_id }, returns { observation_id, status:'approved' }. Returns 409 on non-pending.

files.purpose: Skip pending observation and resume agent.

sections.label: Skip
sections.content: Implements POST skip; validates record is pending then updates to skipped, publishes pending-obs:{pending_id} { status:'skipped' }, returns 200 status payload; returns 409 on non-pending.

files.purpose: List reports without html snapshot URL.

sections.label: List Reports
sections.content: Implements GET /api/reports with filters client_id/status and default sort generated_at DESC. Response item fields exactly: report_id, version, status, language, generated_at, client_id, source_report_id. Explicitly excludes html_snapshot_url to resolve RISK-15.

files.purpose: Get single report with freshly-presigned URLs.

sections.label: Report Detail
sections.content: Implements GET /api/reports/{id}. Generates html_snapshot_url from report.html_snapshot_r2_key with 3600s expiry each request. Returns pdf_url as null when pdf_r2_key null, otherwise presigned 3600s URL. Validates tenant ownership.

files.purpose: Return lineage chain for report versions.

sections.label: Version Chain
sections.content: Implements GET /api/reports/{id}/versions returning ascending version list { report_id, version, source_report_id, generated_at, status, language } by traversing lineage root or querying shared root.

files.purpose: Trigger report regeneration with optional style memory write.

sections.label: Regenerate
sections.content: Implements POST regenerate with body { style_instruction<=500, client_id?, language? }. Validates source ownership, optional mem0 write (5s timeout + 1 retry; continue on failure), resolves chat session from X-Chat-Session-Id header or creates new one, enqueues agent-loop mode=regenerate (pre-seeded observation_ids and derivation_result_ids), returns { report_id, version, session_id, html_snapshot_url, pdf_url|null }. Returns 409 { code:'generation_in_progress' } when active regeneration exists.

files.purpose: Core orchestration loop for tool-calling and report generation.

sections.label: Flow
sections.content: Implements sequence: emit tool_call preferences, get_preferences, identify sections, search_observations then search_evidence fallback, propose_manual_observation + await pending-obs pub/sub max 10 min, compute_derivation rollups, Grok stream tokens, create_report, render_pdf, emit report_ready then done. Errors emit error event and write ChatMessage(type=error).

files.purpose: Stream Grok tokens and persist assistant messages.

sections.label: Streaming
sections.content: Reads token chunks from Grok response stream, emits token SSE events to session buffer, accumulates complete assistant text, writes one chat_message type=agent_text on completion, then done event.

files.purpose: Redis Streams-backed per-session replay buffer.

sections.label: Replay Buffer
sections.content: Uses Redis Stream key sse:chat:{session_id}. Appends events with XADD MAXLEN ~ 200. Replays with XRANGE using Last-Event-ID + 1. Reads live with XREAD BLOCK. Sets EXPIRE 60 after done event.

files.purpose: MCP registry and guarded dispatcher.

sections.label: Dispatch
sections.content: Exports toolDispatch(toolName,input,ctx). Validates toolName exists else returns { error:'unknown_tool' }. Injects ctx.companyId into all tool implementations; tool inputs contain no company_id. Wraps calls in try/catch and propagates retryable vs fatal errors for agent loop.

files.purpose: Fetch approved or filtered observations for agent reasoning.

sections.label: Contract
sections.content: Input { query, filters? }. Output { observations }. Applies tenant scoping and filters category_id, normalized_key, period range, status; returns Observation-compatible records.

files.purpose: Hybrid retrieval for evidence blocks.

sections.label: Contract
sections.content: Input { query, filters? }. Output blocks with block_id, document_version_id, page_number, bbox, text, document_title, category_path, score. Reuses Slice 3 hybrid retrieval (dense+sparse RRF) and scopes by ctx.companyId.

files.purpose: Compute or reuse derivation results with coverage semantics.

sections.label: Contract
sections.content: Input observation_ids + operation + optional expected_periods. Validates approved-only observations, numeric/unit compatibility, ratio non-zero denominator, computes coverage fraction when expected_periods provided, enforces insufficient_coverage signaling below 0.50, caches by SHA256(sorted IDs + operation + companyId), returns status fresh|reused and stale flag.

files.purpose: Create pending manual observation and notify UI.

sections.label: Contract
sections.content: Input suggested fields, creates pending_manual_observation row with expires_at +10 minutes, emits manual_obs_request SSE payload with pending_id and prefilled fields, schedules per-record delayed timeout and relies on repeatable safety sweep.

files.purpose: Persist report metadata and HTML snapshot in R2.

sections.label: Contract
sections.content: Validates html_content <= 2MB, validates observation_ids approved and derivation_result_ids owned by ctx.companyId, uploads HTML to {company_id}/reports/{report_id}/snapshot.html, inserts report row with html_snapshot_r2_key, style_snapshot from mem0 preferences, source_report_id support for regeneration, returns report metadata with fresh html_snapshot_url.

files.purpose: Enqueue PDF rendering and await readiness with timeout fallback.

sections.label: Contract
sections.content: Validates report ownership and html snapshot presence, enqueues render-pdf-job with output key {company_id}/reports/{report_id}/report.pdf, waits via Redis pub/sub render-pdf:{report_id} up to 90s, falls back to polling report.pdf_r2_key every 2s, returns { pdf_url, report_id } or { error:'render_timeout' }.

files.purpose: Return nested category tree to agent.

sections.label: Contract
sections.content: Input {} output categories array with category_id,name,path,parent_category_id,children[], scoped to ctx.companyId.

files.purpose: Bulk merge observation keys/labels.

sections.label: Contract
sections.content: Input observation_ids + canonical fields, updates approved observations in company scope and writes one audit_log row per updated observation, returns updated_count.

files.purpose: Load report plus linked observations and derivations.

sections.label: Contract
sections.content: Input { report_id } output { report, observations, derivations } after tenant validation.

files.purpose: Load mem0 preferences with degraded timeout mode.

sections.label: Contract
sections.content: Input { user_id, client_id? } output { preferences }. Uses scope user_id + company_id + optional client_id. On mem0 read timeout at 3s returns empty object and continues.

files.purpose: PDF worker entry with health endpoint and BullMQ consumer.

sections.label: Worker
sections.content: Bootstraps Redis/BullMQ worker for render-pdf-job, initializes Puppeteer pool, exposes /health on port 3003 internally (Docker maps host port 3001 to container port 3003 per docker-compose, so PDF_WORKER_URL=http://localhost:3001 is correct for callers outside the container) returning { status:'ok', pool_size, active_renders }, and handles graceful shutdown.

files.purpose: Render HTML snapshot to PDF and persist result key.

sections.label: Job Steps
sections.content: Fetch HTML from R2, sanitize via DOMPurify forbidden tags/attrs policy, reject if >5% content stripped, render with Puppeteer page.pdf({format:'A4',printBackground:true}), upload to R2 output_r2_key, update report.pdf_r2_key, publish render-pdf:{report_id} completion message, release browser.

files.purpose: Puppeteer pool lifecycle and timeout controls.

sections.label: Pool
sections.content: Maintains max 3 concurrent browser instances, tracks per-instance render count and restarts after 50 renders, enforces per-render 30s timeout via Promise.race, raises retriable failures for BullMQ attempt handling.

files.purpose: Dedicated deployable image for PDF worker.

sections.label: Container
sections.content: Uses node:20-slim, installs chromium + runtime libs, sets PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH and PUPPETEER_SKIP_DOWNLOAD, installs prod deps, copies worker and lib modules, EXPOSE 3003 (Docker maps host 3001:3003 per docker-compose, so PDF_WORKER_URL=http://localhost:3001 is correct for callers outside the container), defines healthcheck and node --max-old-space-size=512 command.

files.purpose: Chat landing page with session sidebar and active/new session area.

sections.label: UI
sections.content: Renders session list and empty-state prompts. Supports creating a session and navigating to /chat/[sessionId].

files.purpose: Active session chat page.

sections.label: UI
sections.content: Loads session metadata, message history, and mounts ChatShell with SSE stream lifecycle and reconnect semantics.

files.purpose: Reports list UI.

sections.label: UI
sections.content: Displays paginated reports from GET /api/reports with filters client_id/status and French empty state CTA to /chat.

files.purpose: Report detail and regeneration controls.

sections.label: UI
sections.content: Loads report detail with fresh presigned html_snapshot_url and pdf_url, renders sandboxed iframe, version sidebar, regenerate dialog, and 3s polling while pdf_url is null.

files.purpose: Main chat interaction shell.

sections.label: Behavior
sections.content: Maintains messages and streaming state, consumes token/tool_call/manual_obs_request/report_ready/error/done events, manages Last-Event-ID reconnect, disables input while manual popup active, shows typing indicator.

files.purpose: Type-dispatched message renderer.

sections.label: Rendering
sections.content: Renders user_text right aligned plain text, agent_text left markdown with streaming cursor, delegates other message types to specialized cards.

files.purpose: Collapsed card for tool invocation messages.

sections.label: UI
sections.content: Shows tool_name and summary by default with optional expandable details object.

files.purpose: Inline card for manual observation requests.

sections.label: UI
sections.content: Displays pending requirement and CTA opening ManualObsPopup for pending_id.

files.purpose: Card for completed report generation.

sections.label: UI
sections.content: Provides 'Voir le rapport' navigation and PDF download action if pdf_url present.

files.purpose: List and create chat sessions.

sections.label: UI
sections.content: Renders paginated sessions with updated_at ordering and button 'Nouvelle conversation'.

files.purpose: Modal form for pending manual observation completion.

sections.label: Form
sections.content: Implements all popup fields/validations, 10-minute countdown, confirm and skip actions, banner state for disabled chat input, timeout toast auto-close behavior.

files.purpose: Safe report HTML preview component.

sections.label: Security
sections.content: Renders iframe with sandbox='allow-scripts allow-same-origin' and never uses dangerouslySetInnerHTML.

files.purpose: Version lineage and regeneration entry point.

sections.label: UI
sections.content: Lists report versions ordered ascending and provides regenerate dialog trigger with style_instruction input.

validation_rules: user_id and company_id derived from Auth.js JWT claims
implementation_notes: Uses src/lib/auth.ts::auth() for ctx extraction with JWT shape { user_id, company_id, role }.
Insert targets drizzle/migrations/0006_report_chat_notification.sql::chat_session and respects char_length(title)<=200.
DB calls wrapped with src/lib/db/rls.ts::withTenant to enforce tenant policy from drizzle/migrations/0007_async_audit_rls.sql::tenant_isolation.

validation_rules: user can list only own sessions
implementation_notes: Reads from drizzle/migrations/0006_report_chat_notification.sql::chat_session and ::chat_message with index (user_id, created_at DESC).
Sort defaults to updated_at DESC as specified in Slice 5 §5.3.
Depends on Slice 1 schema file src/lib/db/schema/notifications.ts symbols for ChatSession and ChatMessage tables.

error_responses.condition: Session missing or not owned by user

implementation_notes: Relies on FK cascade from drizzle/migrations/0006_report_chat_notification.sql::chat_message.session_id ON DELETE CASCADE.
Ownership check via chat_session.user_id = ctx.userId (Slice 5 §5.17).

validation_rules: title update only for first message
response must be immediate async dispatch

error_responses.condition: Session missing or not owned by user

implementation_notes: Writes chat_message with type user_text using shape from Slice 5 §5.13 and enum drizzle/migrations/0001_foundation.sql::chat_message_type user_text.
Asynchronous agent dispatch resolves RISK-03 and depends on src/lib/agent/loop.ts::runAgentLoop.
Queue timeout alignment references src/lib/queues.ts queue defaults from Slice 1 §1.7 (all six queue timeouts).

validation_rules: content shape validated against type-discriminated schema
implementation_notes: RISK-09 resolution uses strict per-type content contracts from Slice 5 §5.13.
Reads from Slice 1 table definition drizzle/migrations/0006_report_chat_notification.sql::chat_message(content jsonb).
Canonical public contract remains /api/chat/sessions/{id}/messages per Slice 5 §5.3 and §5.19 reconnect fallback behavior.
Implements Redis Streams replay using XADD/XRANGE/XREAD on key sse:chat:{session_id} per Slice 5 §5.4.
Uses src/lib/redis.ts::redis and dedicated subscriber client pattern introduced in Slice 1 §1.7.
This supersedes Slice 2 in-memory replay model for chat stream and resolves RISK-03 under horizontal scaling.

validation_rules: pending record must belong to ctx.companyId
implementation_notes: Uses Slice 1 table drizzle/migrations/0007_async_audit_rls.sql::pending_manual_observation.
RISK-04 response shape enforced exactly with status and observation_id semantics.
Ownership guard mirrors Slice 5 §5.17 PendingManualObservation security requirement.

validation_rules: pending_id exists and status is pending
period_start and period_end required when time_behavior=periodic
category_id if present belongs to ctx.companyId
implementation_notes: Creates observation and attestation using Slice 1 symbols: drizzle/migrations/0004_fact_layer.sql::observation and ::attestation_record.
Uses enum values from drizzle/migrations/0001_foundation.sql::data_type_enum and ::time_behavior_enum.
Publishes pub/sub update to pending-obs:{pending_id} so src/lib/agent/loop.ts waiting branch unblocks immediately.

validation_rules: pending_id exists and status is pending
implementation_notes: Updates Slice 1 table drizzle/migrations/0007_async_audit_rls.sql::pending_manual_observation status and emits pending-obs channel message.
Agent treats skipped and timed_out identically per Slice 5 §5.8 fallback logic.
Uses Slice 1 table drizzle/migrations/0006_report_chat_notification.sql::report.
RISK-15 resolution explicitly omits html_snapshot_url from list response and avoids presign generation at list time.
Client filters align with Slice 6 client CRUD entity introduced in src/app/api/clients routes.

validation_rules: pdf_url null allowed when rendering incomplete

error_responses.condition: Report missing or out of tenant scope

implementation_notes: Presign source keys: drizzle/migrations/0006_report_chat_notification.sql::report.html_snapshot_r2_key and ::report.pdf_r2_key.
R2 client dependency from src/lib/r2.ts singleton established in Slice 1.
Front-end recovers expired report_ready URLs by re-calling this endpoint as defined in Slice 5 §5.13.

validation_rules: report lineage must be within ctx.companyId

error_responses.condition: Lineage root cannot be resolved in tenant scope

implementation_notes: Lineage uses self FK drizzle/migrations/0006_report_chat_notification.sql::report.source_report_id.
Acceptance in Slice 5 requires chain output after regeneration with v1 null source and v2 referencing v1.
Depends on Slice 1 tables chat_session and report for session provisioning + version linkage.
Regeneration writes mem0 preferences under scope model introduced in Slice 4 and §8 memory architecture.
Dispatches to BullMQ queue agent-loop out-of-band while render-pdf-job uses timeout 90_000 from src/lib/queues.ts constants table in Slice 1 §1.7.
Pipeline SSE notification event type report_ready from Slice 4 §4.7 can short-circuit report detail polling in UI.

background_jobs.trigger: POST /api/chat/sessions/{id}/messages and POST /api/reports/{id}/regenerate dispatch asynchronous agent execution per Slice 5 §5.3 and §5.12.

steps.label: Load context
steps.detail: Load chat_session and authenticated context, then enforce ownership with chat_session.user_id and tenant scope from src/lib/db/rls.ts::withTenant (Slice 5 §5.17; Slice 1 RLS model).

steps.label: Execute tool loop
steps.detail: Run src/lib/agent/loop.ts orchestration with MCP tools from src/lib/mcp/index.ts; all tool calls derive company scope from AgentContext.companyId (RISK-08, Slice 5 §5.6-§5.7).

steps.label: Handle manual observation waits
steps.detail: If propose_manual_observation is emitted, wait on Redis pub/sub pending-obs:{pending_id} up to 10 minutes; pending-obs-timeout queue provides terminal-state unblocking safety net per Slice 5 §5.8.

steps.label: Generate report artifacts
steps.detail: Call create_report then render_pdf; render_pdf enqueues render-pdf-job and waits with pub/sub plus DB poll fallback up to 90 seconds per Slice 5 §5.7.

steps.label: Stream and persist outputs
steps.detail: Append SSE events to Redis Stream sse:chat:{session_id} (XADD/XRANGE/XREAD) and persist final ChatMessage content shapes from Slice 5 §5.13.

error_handling: On non-retryable tool failure, emit SSE error event and persist ChatMessage(type=error, content={ message, retryable }) as defined in Slice 5 §5.13 and §5.19.
On render timeout, return { error:'render_timeout' } from render_pdf path and continue with HTML snapshot fallback per Slice 5 §5.7 and §5.19.

background_jobs.trigger: MCP tool render_pdf called from agent loop after create_report completes.

steps.label: Load report and HTML
steps.detail: Verify report exists in ctx.companyId and has html_snapshot_r2_key. Fetch snapshot HTML from R2.

steps.label: Sanitize HTML
steps.detail: Run DOMPurify with WHOLE_DOCUMENT true, forbid tags script/iframe/object/embed/form/input/textarea/select and event attrs onerror/onload/onclick/onmouseover, ALLOW_DATA_ATTR false. If removed content ratio > 5%, fail as malicious payload.

steps.label: Acquire browser from pool
steps.detail: Obtain or spawn Puppeteer instance from pool (max 3). Enforce per-render timeout 30s with Promise.race.

steps.label: Render PDF
steps.detail: page.setContent(cleanHtml) with networkidle0 wait, then page.pdf({ format:'A4', printBackground:true }).

steps.label: Persist PDF
steps.detail: Upload buffer to R2 output key {company_id}/reports/{report_id}/report.pdf.

steps.label: Update DB and notify
steps.detail: UPDATE report SET pdf_r2_key = output_r2_key WHERE report_id = ?. Publish Redis pub/sub message to render-pdf:{report_id} with { pdf_r2_key }.

steps.label: Release pool resources
steps.detail: Return browser to pool; increment render counter and recycle instance after 50 renders.

error_handling: On per-render timeout, throw retriable failure for BullMQ retry.
After two failed attempts, job goes failed; report.pdf_r2_key remains null.
MCP render_pdf waits max 90s then returns { error:'render_timeout' } and agent falls back to html_snapshot_url.

background_jobs.trigger: Repeatable safety-net job every 300000 ms plus delayed per-record timeout scheduling.

steps.label: Query expired pending records
steps.detail: SELECT * FROM pending_manual_observation WHERE status = 'pending' AND expires_at < now() (drizzle/migrations/0007_async_audit_rls.sql::pending_manual_observation).

steps.label: Transition to timed_out
steps.detail: For each row, UPDATE status = 'timed_out' as terminal state (maps to pending_obs_status enum lifecycle from Slice 1 + Slice 5 §5.8).

steps.label: Unblock waiting agent
steps.detail: Publish Redis message { status:'timeout' } to pending-obs:{pending_id} so src/lib/agent/loop.ts resumes without polling (Slice 5 §5.8).

steps.label: Operational logging
steps.detail: Log timed-out count for observability and worker diagnostics.

error_handling: Sweep retries on transient DB/Redis failures.
Repeatable sweep exists even when primary delayed jobs are lost due to Redis restart or worker crash (MR-28).

background_jobs.trigger: Scheduled when propose_manual_observation creates a pending record.

steps.label: Load target row
steps.detail: Read pending_manual_observation by pending_id (drizzle/migrations/0007_async_audit_rls.sql) and confirm status is pending.

steps.label: Apply timed_out
steps.detail: If still pending and now >= expires_at, set status to timed_out.

error_handling: If row already confirmed/skipped/timed_out, no-op and complete.

types_and_interfaces.name: AgentContext
types_and_interfaces.definition: type AgentContext = { companyId: string; userId: string; sessionId: string; };

types_and_interfaces.name: ChatMessageContent
ChatSseEvent
ComputeDerivationInputOutput
CreateReportInputOutput
types_and_interfaces.definition:
type CreateReportInput = { client_id?: string; language: string; html_content: string; observation_ids: string[]; derivation_result_ids: string[]; reporting_period_start?: string; reporting_period_end?: string };
type CreateReportOutput = { report_id: string; version: number; html_snapshot_url: string; style_snapshot: Record<string, unknown> | null; status: 'draft' };

types_and_interfaces.name: RenderPdfInputOutput
types_and_interfaces.definition:
type RenderPdfInput = { report_id: string };
type RenderPdfOutput = { report_id: string; pdf_url: string } | { error: 'render_timeout' };

types_and_interfaces.name: PendingManualObservationApi
ManualObservationConfirmRequest
types_and_interfaces.definition: type ManualObservationConfirmRequest = { label: string; normalized_key: string; value: string; unit?: string | null; data_type: 'numeric' | 'percentage' | 'text' | 'boolean'; time_behavior: 'periodic' | 'point_in_time' | 'none'; period_start?: string | null; period_end?: string | null; category_id?: string | null; source_reference?: string | null; note?: string | null; };

types_and_interfaces.name: ReportsApiShapes

constants.name: PROJECT_STACK
constants.value: PostgreSQL 16 + pgvector + pg_trgm; Drizzle ORM; BullMQ + Redis; Cloudflare R2; Next.js 14 App Router; Auth.js credentials+JWT; PaddleOCR-VL 1.5; Grok grok-4-1-fast-reasoning; OpenAI text-embedding-3-small; mem0; TypeScript strict mode
constants.purpose: Cross-cutting architecture context that must be preserved in Slice 5 implementation.

constants.name: THREE_PROCESS_DEPLOYMENT
constants.value: Next.js app:3000 | pipeline worker health:3002 | PDF worker health:3003

constants.name: AUTH_JWT_SHAPE
constants.purpose: Tenant and RBAC context source for all route and tool guards.

constants.name: BULLMQ_QUEUE_TIMEOUTS
constants.value: ocr-job=180000; embedding-job=120000; extraction-job=300000; semantic-conflict-job=60000; render-pdf-job=90000; pending-obs-timeout=30000
constants.purpose: Timeout policy alignment across app and worker processes.

constants.name: CHAT_SSE_STREAM_MAXLEN
constants.purpose: Approximate maximum replay entries per session stream via XADD MAXLEN ~ 200.

constants.name: CHAT_SSE_STREAM_TTL_SECONDS
constants.purpose: Replay retention after done event for reconnect gap handling.

constants.name: MANUAL_OBS_EXPIRY_MINUTES
constants.purpose: Pending manual observation terminal timeout window.

constants.name: REPORT_HTML_MAX_BYTES
constants.purpose: Maximum HTML snapshot size (2 MB) enforced before R2 upload.

constants.name: PDF_RENDER_TIMEOUT_SECONDS
constants.purpose: Total wait budget for pub/sub + DB poll fallback before render_timeout response.

constants.name: PDF_POOL_RESTART_AFTER_RENDERS
constants.purpose: Prevent long-lived Chromium memory leak accumulation.

constants.name: PDF_PER_RENDER_TIMEOUT_MS
constants.purpose: Per-page render timeout bound inside worker.

acceptance_criteria: POST /api/chat/sessions/{id}/messages returns 202 immediately and agent output is asynchronous over SSE.
GET /api/chat/sessions/{id}/stream emits token events then done and supports Last-Event-ID replay without losing events across reconnect.
propose_manual_observation emits manual_obs_request; popup opens prefilled; confirm creates approved observation and resumes chat.
create_report uploads HTML snapshot to R2 and persists html_snapshot_r2_key; render_pdf enqueues render-pdf-job and eventually emits report_ready.
GET /api/reports excludes html_snapshot_url in list response.
GET /api/reports/{id} returns fresh presigned html_snapshot_url valid for one hour and pdf_url null-or-url depending on readiness.
ReportDetail renders HTML via sandboxed iframe and does not use dangerouslySetInnerHTML.
GET /api/reports/{id}/versions returns lineage chain with correct source_report_id references after regeneration.
GET /api/manual-observations/pending/{id} returns pending status payload fields exactly as specified.
POST /api/manual-observations/pending/{id}/confirm returns 409 for non-pending terminal records.
All MCP tools derive company scope from AgentContext.companyId and do not accept company_id in tool input.
PDF worker builds/runs independently and processes render-pdf-job even when Next.js app process is unavailable.

known_spec_deviations.item: Slice 5 states no new migrations but still relies on report/html key and pending lifecycle semantics
known_spec_deviations.reason: All required entities are present from Slice 1 migrations 0004/0006/0007; this slice performs behavioral logic only and requires no additional DDL.

known_spec_deviations.item: pending_obs_status DB enum value is `timed_out` (SQL) but API/response layer exposes it as `timeout` per §5.8 contract. Both are intentional — DB layer and API layer use different tokens.
known_spec_deviations.reason: SQL enum pending_obs_status (§1.4) uses `timed_out`; §5.8 API response shape and lifecycle transitions specify `timeout` as the client-visible token. Background job DB-write steps write `timed_out` to match the SQL enum; api_endpoints response bodies, SSE payloads, and type definitions expose `timeout` to match the §5.8 contract.

known_spec_deviations.item: agent-loop timeout_ms is 0 (unlimited) rather than a bounded value
known_spec_deviations.reason: The agent loop must wait sequentially for pending manual observation responses (up to 10 minutes each), report generation, and PDF rendering (90s). A fixed timeout would risk killing legitimate long sessions. BullMQ's individual job step timeouts and the pending-obs 10-minute TTL provide bounded sub-task constraints instead.

known_spec_deviations.item: PDF worker listens on internal port 3003 while PDF_WORKER_URL uses port 3001
known_spec_deviations.reason: The PDF worker process binds to port 3003 inside its container (EXPOSE 3003 in Dockerfile, /health endpoint on 3003 in index.ts). Docker-compose maps host port 3001 to container port 3003 (ports: 3001:3003), so callers outside the container (e.g. the Next.js app) correctly use PDF_WORKER_URL=http://localhost:3001. There is no conflict — this is standard Docker port mapping.

implementation_order: Wire chat session CRUD and message APIs with ownership guards and strict content validation.
Implement Redis Streams session buffer and SSE stream endpoint with Last-Event-ID replay semantics.
Implement MCP dispatcher and foundational tools get_preferences/search_observations/search_evidence/compute_derivation.
Implement propose_manual_observation plus pending GET/confirm/skip endpoints and pub/sub unblocking flow.
Implement create_report tool and report list/detail/versions endpoints with fresh presigned URL behavior.
Implement PDF worker (pool, render job, Dockerfile) and MCP render_pdf wait/poll fallback.
Implement report regeneration endpoint with optional mem0 write and session resolution strategy.
Implement chat UI shell/cards/popup and reports list/detail/version sidebar with polling + notification short-circuit.
Add unit/integration/E2E tests for replay buffer, tool dispatch, pending lifecycle, report URL contract, and regenerate lineage.
Run verification checklist: typecheck, tests, curl contract checks, docker build workers/pdf, and end-to-end chat→report flow under 90s fixture target.
